title: Koeleria hirsuta
intro: Koeleria hirsuta, also known as hairy June grass, is a perennial grass native to Europe and Asia, belonging to the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its health benefits include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Koeleria hirsuta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Koeleria hirsuta is typically used to prepare herbal teas and infusions, as well as tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin problems.
taxonomy: Koeleria hirsuta is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It is a member of the tribe Poeae and the subfamily Pooideae. The genus Koeleria is a part of the subfamily Pooideae within the family Poaceae. This species is native to Europe and Western Asia. It is a perennial grass that typically grows in dry and sandy areas.
uses: Koeleria hirsuta is a species of grass, but information about its specific medicinal uses is limited.
